tex:
(a) The list shows 15 midday temperatures, in degrees Celsius, in Suntown.
\[17 \quad 21 \quad 21 \quad 18 \quad 23 \quad 22 \quad 25 \quad 19\]
\[21 \quad 17 \quad 19 \quad 18 \quad 21 \quad 24 \quad 23\]

(i)  Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram to show this information.

\begin{tabular}{r|l}
1 & 7 \\
\hline
2 & \\
\end{tabular}

Key: $1|7$ represents $17°$C

 Find the median.

 Find the upper quartile.

 Rahul draws a pie chart to show this information.

Calculate the sector angle for the number of days the temperature is $18°$C.

(b) 

The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the masses, in grams, of some apples.

(i)  Find the median.

 Find the range.

 Find the interquartile range.

(c) (i)  The time, $t$ minutes, spent on homework in one week by each of 200 students is recorded.\\
The table shows the results.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $40 < t \leqslant 60$ & $60 < t \leqslant 80$ & $80 < t \leqslant 90$ & $90 < t \leqslant 100$ & $100 < t \leqslant 150$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 6 & 10 & 70 & 84 & 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate an estimate of the mean.

 A new table with different class intervals is completed.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $40 < t \leqslant 90$ & $90 < t \leqslant 150$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 86 & 114 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
On a histogram the height of the bar for the $40 < t \leqslant 90$ interval is 17.2\,cm.

Calculate the height of the bar for the $90 < t \leqslant 150$ interval.

tex:
(a) Alex, Bobbie and Chris share strawberries in the ratio Alex : Bobbie : Chris $= 3 : 2 : 2$.\\
Chris receives 12 strawberries.

Calculate the total number of strawberries shared.

(b) In a sale, a shop reduces all prices by 12\%.

(i)  Dina buys a book which has an original price of \$6.50.

Calculate how much Dina pays for the book.

 Elu pays \$11 for a toy.

Calculate the original price of the toy.

(c) Feri invests some money.\\
The rate of interest for the first year is 2.5\%.\\
At the end of the second year the overall percentage increase of Feri's investment is 6.6\%.

Find the rate of interest for the second year.

(d) A radioactive substance decays at an exponential rate of 2\% per day.\\
The initial mass is 80\,g.

(i)  Find the mass at the end of 5 days.

 Find how many **more** whole days, after day 5, it takes for the mass to reduce to less than 67\,g.

(a) Geeta buys $x$ apples, $(x + 7)$ oranges and $(2x - 1)$ bananas.\\
The total number of pieces of fruit Geeta buys is 30.

(i)  Find the number of apples Geeta buys.

 The cost of one apple is 15 cents.\\
The cost of one orange is 18 cents.\\
The total cost of all the fruit is \$5.55.

Find the cost, in cents, of one banana.

(b) (i)  Solve.
\[\frac{3w}{16} - 1 = \frac{1}{2}\]

 $\dfrac{3(2^{-y})}{16} - 1 = \dfrac{1}{2}$

Find the value of $y$.

(c) (i)  Solve the simultaneous equations.
\begin{align*}
2p + q &= 2 \\
p - q &= -\frac{1}{2}
\end{align*}

 Hence, for $0° \leqslant u \leqslant 360°$ and $0° \leqslant v \leqslant 360°$, solve the simultaneous equations.
\begin{align*}
2\sin u + \cos v &= 2 \\
\sin u - \cos v &= -\frac{1}{2}
\end{align*}

{...}]

{...}]

\[f(x) = 2x - 1 \qquad g(x) = 3x - 2 \qquad h(x) = \frac{1}{x}, \, x \neq 0 \qquad j(x) = 5^x\]

(a) Find

(i)  $f(2)$,

 $gf(2)$.

(b) Find $g^{-1}(x)$.

(c) Find $x$ when $h(x) = j(-2)$.

(d) Write $f(x) - h(x)$ as a single fraction.

(e) Find the value of $jj(2)$.

(f) Find $x$ when $j^{-1}(x) = 4$.

(a) $ABCDEFGH$ is a regular octagon with sides of length 6\,cm.\\
The diagram shows part of the octagon.\\
$O$ is the centre of the octagon and $M$ is the midpoint of $AB$.

(i)  \begin{parts}

(b) Show that angle $OAM$ is $67.5°$.

(c) Calculate the area of the octagon.

 Find the area of the circle that passes through the vertices of the octagon.

The diagram shows a horizontal container for water with a uniform cross-section.\\
The cross-section is a semicircle.\\
The radius of the semicircle is 0.45\,m and the length of the container is 4\,m.

(i)  Calculate the volume of the container.

 The greatest depth of the water in the container is 0.3\,m.\\
The diagram shows the cross-section.

Calculate the number of litres of water in the container.\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest integer.

\end{parts}

(a) The diagram shows the graph of $y = f(x)$ for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.

(i)  Find $f(2)$.

 Solve the equation $f(x) = 0$ for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.

{...} or $x = $ ]

 $f(x) = k$ has three solutions for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$ where $k$ is an integer.

Find the smallest possible value of $k$.

 On the grid, draw a line $y = mx$ so that $f(x) = mx$ has exactly one solution for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.

(b) $y = 3x^2 - 12x + 7$

(i)  Find the value of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ when $x = 5$.

 Find the coordinates of the point on the graph of $y = 3x^2 - 12x + 7$ where the gradient is 0.

{...} )]

(c) When $y = 2x^p + qx^2$, $\dfrac{dy}{dx} = 14x^6 + 6x$.

Find the value of $p$ and the value of $q$.

(a) Calculate angle $ACD$.

(b) Show that $BC = 7.05$\,km, correct to 2 decimal places.

(c) Calculate the shortest distance from $B$ to $AC$.

(d) Calculate the length of the straight line $BD$.

(e) $C$ is due east of $A$.

Find the bearing of $D$ from $C$.

(a) (i)  Use set notation to describe the shaded region in the Venn diagram.

 Shade the correct region in each Venn diagram.

(b) \begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
V & E & N & N & D & I & A & G & R & A & M \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
The diagram shows 11 cards.

(i)  One of these cards is chosen at random.

Write down the probability that the letter on the card is **not** A.

 A card is chosen at random from these 11 cards and then replaced.\\
A second card is then chosen at random.

Find the probability that exactly one card has the letter N.

(c) 

50 students are asked if they like English ($E$) and if they like mathematics ($M$).\\
3 say they do not like English and do not like mathematics.\\
33 say they like English.\\
42 say they like mathematics.

(i)  Complete the Venn diagram.

 A student is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this student likes English and likes mathematics.

 Two students are chosen at random.

Find the probability that they both like mathematics.

 Two students who like English are chosen at random.

Find the probability that they both also like mathematics.
texHtml:
(a) (i) Use set notation to describe the shaded region in the Venn diagram. Shade the correct region in each Venn diagram. (b)
VENNDIAGRAM
The diagram shows 11 cards. (i) One of these cards is chosen at random. Write down the probability that the letter on the card is **not** A. A card is chosen at random from these 11 cards and then replaced.\\ A second card is then chosen at random. Find the probability that exactly one card has the letter N. (c) 50 students are asked if they like English ($E$) and if they like mathematics ($M$).\\ 3 say they do not like English and do not like mathematics.\\ 33 say they like English.\\ 42 say they like mathematics. (i) Complete the Venn diagram. A student is chosen at random. Find the probability that this student likes English and likes mathematics. Two students are chosen at random. Find the probability that they both like mathematics. Two students who like English are chosen at random. Find the probability that they both also like mathematics.

tex:
(a) 

The area of the rectangle is 29\,cm$^2$ greater than the area of the square.\\
The difference between the perimeters of the two shapes is $k$\,cm.

Find the value of $k$.\\
You must show all your working.

(b) 

The volume of the larger cube is 5\,cm$^3$ greater than the volume of the smaller cube.

(i)  Show that $3y^2 + 3y - 4 = 0$.

 Find the volume of the smaller cube.\\
Show all your working and give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
